title: Annual program statistics - Canada.ca
content:
Scientific Research and Experimental Development (SR&ED) tax incentives

## Annual program statistics
Annual statistics on the SR&ED tax incentive program.
The SR&ED tax incentive program is the largest Government of Canada program supporting research and development in Canada.
Claims that we process in a fiscal period may have been filed in a prior fiscal period. As a result, they may exceed the claims filed in that fiscal period in quantity and investment tax credits.
The data included on this page comes from the Compliance Programs Branch’s Integras case management system and may not match with the Canada Revenue Agency’s assessing systems due to keying errors.
- 90% accepted as filed
- 6% accepted after modifications
- 95% of claims accepted as filed during workload selection were processed within 60 days
- 92.5% of refundable claims selected for service or review were processed within 180 days

### Percentage of total ITCs allowed by field of science
Bar chart indicating percentage of total investment tax cedits allowed by field of science. Software development 42.6%, Medical sciences and engineering 14.2%, Mechanical engineering 13.5%, Electrical engineering 12.5%, Materials engineering 4.6%, Chemistry or chemical engineering 4.4%, Earth sciences - environmental 4.0%, Agricultural sciences 2.2%, Food processing 1.3%, Civil engineering 0.6%, Other or not available 0.0%.

### Percentage of total processed claims based on gross income of business
Pie chart showing percentage of total processed claims based on gross income of business. 65% of claims come from small businesses that make less than $4 million. 20% of claims come from small to medium-sized businesses that make $4 million to $20 million. 12% of claims come from medium-sized businesses that make $20 million to $250 million. 3% of claims come from large businesses that make more than $250 million.

### Percentage of total ITCs allowed based on gross income of business
Pie chart showing the percentage of investment tax credits allowed based on gross income of business. 33% of credits allowed go to small businesses that make less than $4 million. 23% of credits allowed go to small to medium-sized businesses that make $4 million to $20 million. 22% of credits allowed go to medium-sized businesses that make $20 million to $250 million. 22% of credits allowed go to large businesses that make more than $250 million.

### Percentage of total ITCs allowed based on claim refundability
Pie chart showing the percentage of Investment Tax Credits allowed based on claim refundability. 59% refundable, 41% non-refundable.
